reasoning: The staff_magic_v00 composite shows a pink orb crystal atop an orange-brown handle. The orb is approximately 20-25px, the handle extends ~35px below for a total staff height of ~55-60px. The player reference in the scene is ~32px tall. This places the weapon at ~170-190% of player height, directly violating the 'items NEVER larger than player' rule (weapons expected at 50-75% = 16-24px for pickup representation). Additionally, the prominent white bounding-box background rectangle (~120x80px) visually overwhelms the surrounding tilemap and reads as a UI artifact rather than a scene object. Perspective alignment with top-down is acceptable; the orb design aesthetic is appropriate for a magic staff. Core failure is size — the weapon reads as larger than the player character, breaking diorama scale.
